commit.message:
Split deliverable archetype into team-facing + meta-squad-facing skills

New: skills/deliverable-aggregation/ — meta-squad skill for collecting,
validating, and aggregating deliverables from completed teams. Documents
when to aggregate, validation flow, import hooks.

Updated: skills/deliverable-playbook/ — team-facing only. Removed
aggregate.ts references. Replaced with 'What Happens After Completion'
pointing teams to report status and let leadership handle the rest.

README: component table now shows Actor column (Team vs Meta-squad).

The archetype provides tooling for BOTH actors but clearly separates
the responsibilities: teams produce, leadership aggregates.

repository.name: metaPyScape
repository.description: Python client library for MetaboScape

commit.message:
fix: use npm install instead of npm ci for frontend in CI

The lockfile was generated with npm 11.x but CI runs npm 10.x
(Node 22 default). npm ci strictly validates the lockfile and
fails on optional peer dependency resolution differences between
npm versions (esbuild@0.28.0 via vitest/vite 8).

Using npm install is more lenient and handles cross-version
lockfile compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix -o table human-friendly output and ensure -o json is always valid/pipeable (#5)

* Fix -o table human-friendly output and ensure -o json is always valid/pipeable

- output.py: rewrite _print_object_as_kv to recursively expand nested
  dicts/lists so no raw Python repr strings appear in table output
- output.py: move sys import to module level; explicit file=sys.stdout
  and flush() for json format to keep stdout clean for piping to jq
- cli.py: add -o/--output option to get_group so users can write
  'mtbsccli get -o json project <id>' (overrides root-level flag)
- test/test_output.py: 17 new tests covering JSON validity, no extra
  lines, no Python repr strings, nested visibility, and flag override

commit.message:
fix(jetson): bind-mount host TensorRT/PyCUDA into Docker container

L4T runtime image does not include TensorRT Python bindings or PyCUDA.
Instead of trying to install them inside the container (which needs
nvcc/CUDA dev headers not in runtime image), bind-mount the host's
pre-installed packages into the container at runtime.

setup_jetson.sh now:
- Auto-detects host site-packages location for tensorrt/pycuda
- Adds -v bind-mount flags to the Docker run command in systemd service
- Falls back to mounting full dist-packages if specific dirs not found

Also:
- Add ENV PATH/LD_LIBRARY_PATH in Dockerfile for CUDA visibility
- Make check_ready() create DB dir if missing (os.makedirs)
- Revert pycuda from requirements.txt (host-provided, not pip)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add pension chart utilities and type definitions

- Pension chart utils with type definitions and tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: mkForce hyprpaper disable to override nixpalette-hyprland's default

nixpalette-hyprland HM module enables stylix.targets.hyprpaper by default.
Our theming.nix disables it (swww handles wallpaper instead). Use mkForce
to resolve the priority conflict.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Round 2 deep review: 27 fixes + 24 regression tests

Critical fixes:
- writer_slf: try/finally to prevent file descriptor leak on write errors
- server_simulation: readline timeout (120s), async stop handler with
  terminate-wait-force lifecycle, pysource.local.sh validation
- parser_2d: uint64 overflow check, bounds validation, skip <3 face points
- model.py: negative element index check in Mesh2D.__post_init__

High-priority fixes:
- server_import: TelemacFile try/finally for FD safety
- writer_liq: sort + deduplicate BC times before np.interp
- server_analysis: particle tracing 300s timeout, obs metrics cropped
  to overlapping time window (prevents silent RMSE/NSE corruption)
- telemac_tools/__init__: narrowed bare except to specific exception types

Medium fixes:
- server_playback: speed clamped to [0.1, 10.0]
- server_core: layer extraction returns truncated array on failure,
  CRS offset float() with ValueError guard
- crs.py: UTM zone 1-60 validation, zone-missing warning
- validation.py: empty CSV StopIteration catch, .liq column count check
- domain/builder.py: DEM NaN count warning after interpolation
- server_import: NaN coordinate filter in preview layer

Low-severity cleanup:
- analysis.py: removed dead if-False branch
- constants.py: palette KeyError guard with Viridis fallback
- layers.py: arrow scale and contour saddle point comments
- crs.py: docstring correction

Tests: 24 new regression tests in test_round2_fixes.py
Total: 311 tests passing (287 existing + 24 new)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: cover global registry list and update branches in CLI

Add three new integration tests in cli_tests.rs to cover uncovered
branches in main.rs:

- install_global_updates_existing_plugin: triggers the else-branch at
  line 583 ("Updated '{spec}' in global registry") by re-installing an
  already-present plugin, which sets added=false in Registry::install.

- list_global_with_installed_plugins: triggers the else-branch at line
  631 ("Globally installed plugins:") by installing two plugins first,
  then running list --global. Also covers:
  - the true-branch (all engines) at line 636 for a no-engine install
  - the false-branch (engine list join) at line 638 for an --engine install

All four were missed by the single existing list_global_no_installed_plugins
test, which only exercised the empty-registry path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Schema-first workflow for deliverable archetype

Playbook skill:
- Discovery step: now includes data source identification (APIs, repos,
  wikis, telemetry, org charts) → raw/data-sources.json
- Schema Evolution section: schema emerges from first run, evolves with
  domain understanding, becomes a living contract
- Step 3: observe natural data structure during deep-dives
- Step 5: formalize or validate against deliverable.schema.json
- Schema as Skill: once stabilized, create project-level skill documenting
  schema structure, field meanings, quality expectations, edge cases
- Completion criteria: deliverable.schema.json must exist

aggregate.ts:
- --validate flag: validates each deliverable against JSON Schema
- deliverableSchema config field
- Basic structural validation (type, required fields, array items)
- Per-team validation results with item counts
- Invalid deliverables flagged in manifest (validation_warnings)
- branchPrefix default fixed: scan/ → squad/

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: orion-ui
repository.description: Orion is a retro-styled, cross-platform UI framework written in C. The framework is not embedded or mobile-targeted — it is a desktop GUI framework designed for Linux, macOS, and Windows. Its central design philosophy is to faithfully reproduce the Windows API (Win32) message-based programming model in a portable, hardware-accelerated context.

fix: enable nixpalette at HM level to provide stylix.base16Scheme

With autoImport=false, stylix won't propagate image/base16Scheme from
NixOS to HM automatically. nixpalette's HM module handles this, but only
when nixpalette.enable=true at HM level. Mirror theme/userThemeDir from
osConfig so the HM module configures stylix correctly.

fix: security audit — SQL injection, path traversal, error propagation, unused deps (#2)

Security fixes:
- Add validate_sql_identifier() to reject SQL injection via table/column names
- Apply identifier validation in retention purge, export, import, and save_rule
- Validate import path with validate_path_components to prevent directory traversal
- Sanitise org_id in export filename to prevent path traversal

Bug fixes:
- Replace silent .filter_map(|r| r.ok()) with .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()
  in list_snapshots, load_rules, load_org_rules — errors are now propagated
- Reject max_age_days == 0 in save_rule (meaningless/dangerous policy)
- Remove unused dependencies: convergio-security, convergio-telemetry

Tests: 30 → 35 (+5 new security tests)

commit.message:
fix(jetson): match Docker container UID with host agrikd user

Root cause: Dockerfile created agrikd with UID 1000, but host's
agrikd user has UID 2010. Mounted volume files (owned by 2010,
mode 644) were inaccessible to container process (UID 1000).

Fix: Pass HOST_UID/HOST_GID as Docker build args so container user
matches host user exactly. Also chmod 666 existing files in logs/
and data/ dirs during setup to handle re-run scenarios.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: audit improvements - security, bug fixes, and optimizations (#4)

## Bugs Fixed
- DELETE requests now use proper HTTP DELETE method instead of GET (bridge.rs)

## Security
- URL-encode path params and query values to prevent URL injection/path traversal
- Added percent_encode() following RFC 3986 unreserved characters

## Performance
- Reuse HTTP client via OnceLock instead of creating one per request

## Code Quality
- Preserve error details from reqwest send() failures (connect vs timeout vs other)
- Extract shared response handling into handle_response()
- Added 2 new security tests for URL encoding

fix(jetson): fix Docker volume permissions in setup script

Docker container runs as UID created inside the container (agrikd),
which differs from host's agrikd UID. Mounted volumes were only
chown'd to host user, making them inaccessible inside the container.

Fix: After chown, apply chmod to ensure:
- Read-only mounts (config, models): 755 dirs, 644 files
- Read-write mounts (data, logs): 777 dirs
- config.json: 644 after every write (credential injection)
- engine files: 644 after download/conversion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: -j/--json flag, TOTP hidden by default, search via get-logins, clip/totp accept URL (#4)

* fix: UX improvements — -j/--json flag, TOTP visibility, search via get-logins, clip/totp by URL

- Replace global --format {table,json,tsv} with per-subcommand -j/--json flag.
  The flag can be placed anywhere after the subcommand name, which is the
  standard behaviour for CLI tools.

- output.py: TOTP is now hidden in 'show' output unless -p/--show-password is
  passed. TSV format removed (unused).

- search: Changed from get_database_entries + client-side filter to
  get_logins(query), which works without the 'Allow access to all entries'
  permission in KeePassXC Browser Integration settings. The query is matched
  against URLs/hostnames by KeePassXC.

- totp: Now accepts a URL (like 'show') instead of a UUID. The entry is
  resolved via get_logins and the UUID is passed to get_totp internally.

- clip: 'field' is now a positional argument instead of --field.
  New usage: keepassxc-cli clip <password|username|totp> <url>

- pyproject.toml: bump keepassxc-browser-api requirement to >=0.1.3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update README, fix edit to use get_logins, ls permission note, clip error message

- README: rewrite all command examples to match current interface (-j/--json,
  clip <field> <url>, totp <url>, corrected generate options, edit --url required,
  ls permission note, remove tsv references)

- edit: replace get_database_entries with get_logins(url); --url is now required.
  This removes the dependency on the 'Allow access to all entries' KeePassXC
  setting. Current field values are fetched via get_logins so omitted args are
  left unchanged as before.

- ls: add description noting the 'Allow access to all entries' permission required.

- clip: show entry name (not internal UUID) in 'No TOTP configured' error message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update tests for new CLI interfaces

- search: mock get_logins instead of get_database_entries
- edit: use url arg and mock get_logins instead of get_database_entries
- totp: use url arg and add get_logins mock; add test_no_entries case
- output: remove TSV tests (TSV format was removed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* pin api version to 0.1.3

commit.message:
fix: copy static files to dist/ via CopyWebpackPlugin

The express.static middleware references __dirname/public which resolves
to dist/public in production, but webpack wasn't copying src/public/ to
dist/. This meant the GT3 dashboard and push test GUI were missing in
deployed builds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve implement lifecycle debugging and fix prompt (#77)

## Changes

Follows up on findings from debugging #74 / PR #76 (implement loop hit
max iterations).

### Fix prompt improvements
- Rewrote `FIX_PROMPT_TEMPLATE` with explicit self-review guidance:
check for second-order effects, run CI locally, and verify no runtime
artifacts are staged
- Removed generic "use the skill" instruction (skill is already loaded
via `--resume`)

### Lifecycle stats comment
- Implement now posts a stats summary on the PR for **all** terminal
states (complete, partial, max_iterations) — previously only
max_iterations got a comment
- Shows: status, review rounds, premium requests, elapsed time, session
ID
- Posted in the `finally` block so it fires even on partial failures

### Debugging
- Added §7 Debugging section to agent runbook (SSH access, container log
commands, grep patterns)
- `copilot.py` log line now includes `resume=<session_id|none>` for
tracing session continuity

### Docs
- Refreshed stale doc references from prior PRs

commit.message:
Add EXAMPLE.md — complete end-to-end walkthrough

814-line walkthrough showing federation from zero to running teams:
a developer redesigns a portfolio site with federated coding + research
teams. Covers install, setup conversation, onboard, launch, monitor,
directives, completion, knowledge flow, and non-homogeneous second team.

All JSON examples match shipped interfaces (ScanStatus, SignalMessage,
LearningEntry). README links to EXAMPLE.md from Quick Start.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add HTMX template foundation with base layout, home page, and CSS

  responsive meta tags, header/main/footer structure, block definitions
- templates/pages/home.html: home page extending base with welcome section
  and subscription list placeholder for future HTMX loading
- static/css/style.css: minimal CSS with dark mode (prefers-color-scheme),
  responsive card grid, compliance status indicators (green/yellow/red),
  badge components, and HTMX loading indicator styles

commit.message:
fix: remove Nx ESLint rules and resolve all lint warnings

- Rewrite eslint.base.config.mjs to remove @nx/eslint-plugin, use typescript-eslint directly
- Simplify eslint.config.mjs and core/eslint.config.mjs to remove Nx rules
- Add varsIgnorePattern/argsIgnorePattern: '^_' so _-prefixed params are ignored
- Replace non-null assertions with null-safe guards in parser.ts and App.tsx
- Replace non-null assertions with optional chaining/toBeDefined in parser.spec.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: dante-patchbox
repository.description: Dante AoIP matrix mixer and DSP patchbay — Rust + Inferno + axum

commit.message:
feat(phase1): DSP per output — parametric EQ, brick-wall limiter, latency tuning

EQ (crates/patchbox-core/src/dsp/eq.rs):
- 3-band parametric biquad EQ per output
- RBJ Audio EQ Cookbook, transposed direct-form II
- RT-safe: no allocations, change-detects coefficients via sync()

Limiter (crates/patchbox-core/src/dsp/limiter.rs):
- Brick-wall peak limiter per output
- Envelope follower, asymmetric attack/release
- Hard clamp after envelope — true brick wall

Config (config.rs):
- EqBand, EqConfig, LimiterConfig structs with serde defaults
- per_output_eq, per_output_limiter vecs in PatchboxConfig
- rx_jitter_samples, lead_samples now config fields (default 48, 48)
- All new fields backward-compatible via #[serde(default)]

Audio callback (device.rs):
- PerOutputDsp pre-allocated before RT closure — zero allocs in hot path
- EQ -> limiter chain wired into process()
- GR fed to meter state for WebSocket broadcast

API (api.rs):
- 6 new JWT-protected endpoints: GET/PUT /eq/:id, GET/PUT /limiter/:id
- MeterFrame extended with gr_db field
- build.rs: auto-detect PAM library path (Arch/Debian/RHEL)

Web UI (zone.html, zone.js):
- EQ sliders per band (freq, gain, Q)
- Limiter threshold/attack/release controls
- Live GR meter bar via WebSocket

Latency: rx_jitter_samples 192->48, lead_samples 96->48 (~2-3ms total)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
